sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Warren Buffett Has Held American Express for Decades. Should You Own It Too?
feed.summary: Warren Buffett has held American Express (AXP) for decades, showcasing its strong and stable business model. American Express generates revenue primarily from transaction fees, focusing on high-spending customers, and has consistently delivered value through brand loyalty, dividends, and share buybacks. The article suggests that while not a high-growth stock, its core strengths make it a valuable long-term investment.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (CNP) CenterPoint Energy, Inc. Posts Q1 Adjusted EPS $0.56 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $0.55
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy, Inc. (CNP) announced its Q1 adjusted EPS came in at $0.56 per share, surpassing the FactSet estimate of $0.55. The company also expects its 2026 non-GAAP EPS to range from $1.89 to $1.91, aligning with analyst estimates. This financial update was provided by MT Newswires.

feed.title: PulteGroup (PHM) Misses Q1 EPS by 3c, Misses on Revenue
feed.summary: PulteGroup (NYSE: PHM) reported its Q1 earnings, missing analyst estimates for both earnings per share (EPS) and revenue. The company posted an EPS of $1.79 against an estimate of $1.82, and revenue came in at $3.3 billion compared to a consensus estimate of $3.39 billion.

feed.title: PG&E stock up 2% on earnings beat, reaffirmed guidance
feed.summary: PG&E Corporation exceeded analyst expectations in its first-quarter earnings, reporting adjusted EPS of $0.43 and revenue of $6.88 billion. The company's shares rose by 2.37% in pre-market trading, driven by strong performance, progress in rate reductions, and reaffirmed full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance. PG&E also highlighted efforts in lowering residential electric rates, license renewal for Diablo Canyon Power Plant, and advancements in wildfire risk mitigation.

feed.title: Stryker reports dip in executive and employee pay despite revenue growth
feed.summary: Stryker saw a decline in compensation for both its executives and median employees in 2025, despite strong revenue growth and significant strategic business moves. CEO Kevin Lobo's pay, along with that of other senior leaders, decreased due to smaller cash bonuses and stock incentives. The median employee pay also slightly declined, widening the CEO-to-employee pay ratio.

feed.title: Tool maker Snap-on posts $1.2B in quarterly sales as profit rises
feed.summary: Snap-on Incorporated reported an encouraging first quarter for 2026, with net sales rising 5.8% to $1.2 billion and diluted earnings per share increasing to $4.69. The company saw strong organic sales growth, particularly in its Commercial & Industrial Group and U.S. Tools Group, despite ongoing challenges like supply chain disruptions and global conflicts. Snap-on anticipates continued progress, focusing on expanding its customer base in critical industries and projecting capital expenditures of approximately $100 million for the year.

feed.title: PulteGroup, Inc. Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: PulteGroup, Inc. reported its first quarter 2026 financial results, with net income of $347 million, or $1.79 per share, a decrease from the prior year. Despite market challenges, net new orders increased by 3% to 8,034 homes, reflecting continued demand for homeownership. The company also announced a $1.5 billion increase in its share repurchase authorization, bringing the total to $2.1 billion.

feed.title: Netflix Authorizes $25 Billion Stock Buyback in Bid to Boost Share Price
feed.summary: Netflix's board has authorized an additional $25 billion stock buyback program, without an expiration date, aimed at boosting shareholder value and the company's stock price. This move comes after Netflix backed out of a deal to acquire Warner Bros. and confirmed its commitment to reinvesting in content and returning excess cash to shareholders. The new program supplements a previous authorization from December 2024, with $6.8 billion remaining from the earlier allocation.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley raises Vertiv stock price target on data center growth
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has increased its price target for Vertiv Holdings Co. (NYSE:VRT) to $350 from $285, maintaining an Overweight rating, due to the company's strong position in the data center capital expenditure cycle. Vertiv, which derives over 80% of its revenue from data center operations, reported impressive Q1 2026 results that exceeded analyst expectations, leading other firms like Mizuho and Barclays to also raise their price targets. The stock's valuation reflects its significant 292% return over the past year, though InvestingPro suggests it is currently overvalued relative to its Fair Value.

feed.title: PulteGroup Announces $1.5 Billion Increase to Share Repurchase Authorization
feed.summary: PulteGroup, Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has approved a $1.5 billion increase to its share repurchase authorization, bringing the total current authorization to $2.1 billion. This decision aligns with the company's capital allocation strategy, which includes investing in the business, paying dividends, and returning excess funds to shareholders. Over the past decade, PulteGroup has invested $38 billion into its business and returned $9 billion to shareholders through dividends and repurchases.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy reports strong Q1 2026 results; reiterates full-year 2026 guidance; provides an update on Houston Electric load growth
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy (NYSE: CNP) reported strong first-quarter 2026 results with GAAP diluted EPS of $0.48 and non-GAAP EPS of $0.56, largely driven by growth and regulatory recovery. The company reiterated its full-year 2026 non-GAAP EPS guidance and announced 12.2 gigawatts of firmly committed industrial load for Houston Electric, including 8 gigawatts from data centers expected to be energized by 2029. CenterPoint highlighted its commitment to customer affordability, projecting $4 billion in customer savings over the next decade due to increasing connections to its electric systems.

feed.title: Nio's third-gen ES8 reaches 100,000 deliveries as Beijing Auto Show looms
feed.summary: Nio has achieved a significant milestone, delivering its 100,000th third-generation ES8 just before the 2026 Beijing Auto Show. The ES8 has been a strong performer, dominating the large SUV market in Beijing and contributing significantly to Nio's deliveries and financial turnaround. As Nio prepares for an intensive period of new product launches, including the ES9 and Onvo L80, the company aims to maintain this sales momentum despite anticipated Q2 industry pressures.
